title: [CI:DOCS] move footnotes on divisive language to exactly where divisive language is used
body:
My commit is not signed or using a realname but I don't believe that the changes I've made can be subject to copyright.

I got a bit confused when reading the manpage as the footnote on problematic and divisive language appeared after a sentence that doesn't use problematic and divisive language and I think moving the footnote to just after the wording deemed problematic makes the manpage more readable

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *krumelmonster* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [baude](https://github.com/baude) for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@krumelmonster good catch! Thank you for your PR.  How would you like to proceed with it? Our commit guidelines enforce the signing requirement. It looks like you're aware of that, but choose not to sign your commit.

Sorry, I was unaware of these requirements until I opened the pr and I still don't fully understand them.
I chose to make a pr instead of an issue because with other projects I've had the experience that when the solution is clear, that prs are preferred.
I only wanted to suggest this change, I don't deem it copyrightable, anyone is invited to re-do the changes and sign it off themselves. Feel free to close the pr, I don't care

IIUC you do not wish to add a `Signed-off-by` with a name traceable to you. We respect your choice, and appreciate your understanding that we cannot accept your PR without a signature.

In the process of reviewing your contribution I discovered that some of our man pages are an unreadable mess. I am in the process of fixing those. If you wish to close this PR, I will incorporate your suggested changes into my upcoming PR.

Thank you again for finding these issues and for your efforts to fix them.

Just for future reference: krumelmonster is a name "traceable" to me: Unlike with my passport name, you can [track](https://github.com/krumelmonster) [my](https://de.libreoffice.org/about-us/credits/) [contributions](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/fileshelter) [to](https://codeberg.org/tectogen/tectogen) open source under the name krumelmonster. The Podman contribution guidelines currently require "no pseudonyms or anonymous contributions". I respect that. In the podman irc channel, it was mentioned to me that other projects, Linux and the CNCF, have clarified that pseudonyms do qualify as real names and therefore meet the requirement against anonymous contributions.

https://github.com/cncf/foundation/blob/659fd32c86dc/dco-guidelines.md
https://github.com/torvalds/linux/commit/d4563201f33a022fc0353033d9dfeb1606a88330

To me the wording in podmans Contribution guide sounds like a requirement for a passport name and that I don't use on the internet.
